title: Conditional Choice-Set Expert Instead Of Plain Binary Classification
summary: Pit decisions are often relative choices among drivers available on a lap, not independent Bernoulli events.
rationale: Pit decisions are often relative choices among drivers available on a lap, not independent Bernoulli events. Discrete-choice and Plackett-Luce models are designed for grouped alternatives and partial rankings, which is structurally closer to “who pits now?” than rowwise classification.
implementation_hint: Build a compact expert where each race-lap is a choice set. Use conditional logit / conditional multinomial logit or a Plackett-Luce-style approximation on lap-level grouped candidates, with features expressed as relative-to-lap or relative-to-cohort deltas. Convert fitted group probabilities back to per-row scores and blend only on ambiguous slices.
expected_effect: May capture field-relative utility and competition effects with different bias than boosted trees, improving ensemble diversity.
risk: Choice-set misspecification is the main trap: some laps have zero or multiple pitters, and naive independence assumptions can break calibration.
